semanticCore: Deflowering, touching, defiling, menstruating, or physical contact
quranicRelevance: Paradise maidens untouched by man or jinn (Yatmith-hunna - 'lam yatmith-hunna inson').

keyDerivations.meaning: To touch, deflower, make physical contact, defile, or intimate pass

figurativeOrRareUsages: لَمْ يَطْمِثْهُنَّ إِنْسٌ قَبْلَهُمْ وَلَا جَانٌّ (55:74): Untouched/undeflowered by any human before them nor any jinn (pure companion creation in Paradise).
summaryEn: The root represents physical touch and deflowering (Tamth), emphasizing the absolute purity and exclusive companionship of Paradise maidens.
summaryAr: يمثل هذا الجذر البكارة والمس والوطء الحسي الأول (طمث)، مؤكداً نقاء وبكارة حور العين في الجنات كنعيم خاص لأصحاب اليمين (لم يطمثهن إنس).
rawText: طَامِثٌ , (S, Msb, K,) without ة, (Msb, TA,) A woman, (S, Msb, K,) or, accord. to Lh, peculiarly a girl, or young woman, (TA,) menstruating: (S, Msb, K, TA:) or, as some say, for the first time. (Msb, TA.) طَمَثَتْ 1 , (S, Msb, K,) aor. ﹹ, (S, K,) or ﹻ, (Msb,) inf. n. طَمْثٌ; (Msb, TA;) and طَمِثَتْ, (S, Msb, K,) aor. ﹷ; (Msb, K; [accord. to the former of which, the inf. n. of the latter verb seems to be طَمَثٌ; but accord. to the K, it seems to be طَمْثٌ;]) She menstruated; said of a woman: (S, Msb, K:) the primary signification, accord. to Th; that of “ devirgination, ” i. e. “ coition with the causing to bleed, ” being one subsequently given to طَمْثٌ: (TA:) or, as some say, for the first time: (Msb, TA:) and accord. to Lh, used peculiarly in relation to a girl, or young woman. (TA.) ___ طَمَثَهَا, aor. ﹻ (S, Mgh, Msb, K) and ﹹ, (S, Msb, K,) the former accord. to most of the readers in the Kur [lv. 56 and 74], (TA,) inf. n. طَمْثٌ, (S, Msb,) He devirginated her, (Fr, S, Mgh, Msb, K,) namely, a woman, (Mgh,) or his wife, (Msb,) causing her to bleed; (Fr, Mgh, Msb;) not otherwise: (Msb:) or, accord. to some, i. q. جَامَعَهَا, in a general sense: (TA:) in this sense the verb is used in the Kur; (Msb;) or, as some say, in the sense expl. in the next sentence: (TA:) and طُمِثَتْ, aor. تُطْمَثُ, she was caused to bleed by devirgination. (AHeyth, TA.) === طَمَثَ, (AA, S, TA,) inf. n. طَمْثٌ, (AA, S, K, TA,) signifies also ‡ He, or it, touched a thing: (AA, S, K, * TA:) said in relation to anything that is touched: one says, مَا طَمَثَ ذَا المَرْتَعَ قَبْلَنَا أَحَدٌ ‡ No one touched this place of pasturing, or this pasture, before us: and مَا طَمَثَ هٰذِهِ النَّاقَةَ حَبْلٌ قَطُّ ‡ A rope such as is called عِقَال never touched this she-camel: (AA, S, TA:) and هٰذَا جَمَلٌ مَا طَمَثَهُ حَبْلٌ قَطُّ ‡ This is a camel which a rope has never touched. (TA.) === And طَمَثَ البَعِيرَ, inf. n. طَمْثٌ † He bound the camel's fore shank to his (the camel's) arm. (TA.) طَمْثٌ Blood: (Fr, TA: [the context in the TA seems to indicate that it means blood that flows when a woman, or girl, is devirginated:] or the blood of the menses; (KL, and TA in art. جزر;) as also 🡻طِمْثٌ. (KL.) === And Dirt, filth, or pollution. (K.) === And A thing that induces suspicion, or evil opinion: one says, مَا بِفُلَانٍ طَمْثٌ There is not, in such a one, anything that induces suspicion, or evil opinion. (TA.) === And Corruption. (L, K; but not in the CK.) طِمْثٌ : see the next preceding paragraph.
